prep.summary: Your week centers on critical systemic escalations—specifically the Second Line's unilateral override of your compliance rating to "insufficient"—and managing a massive container vulnerability spike from 25,000 to 42,000. You are also steering complex tooling transitions—navigating the migration from Zoom and Slack to Microsoft Teams, defining the boundary between Scan.ai and Celonis, and reviewing Sierra's conversational AI capabilities. These parallel efforts highlight a central trend—balancing urgent risk remediation with long-term foundational capability building across the enterprise.
tasks: Challenge the Second Line compliance team to provide a concrete remediation path—specifically targeting WIAM and PAM streams—to shield operational risk calculations from the "insufficient" rating.
Partner with Chain Guard to build hardened application-tier images—targeting open-source frameworks like Netty and Tomcat—to drive down the massive influx of 42,000 container vulnerabilities.
Draft the high-level one-pager for leadership—utilizing the Celonis representative's email verification—to establish Scan.ai as a distinct, net-new capability for desktop task mining.
Track the transition to the Microsoft Enterprise ecosystem—specifically resolving the post-Zoom whiteboarding gap—to ensure collaboration continuity for the P&C Architecture team.
prep.title: Tech Fellow Standup

events.summary: Artifacts to support Products and Services
